steps.title: Spot the contrast in distances
steps.content: The passage first establishes JWST's distance from Earth (one million miles), then introduces Hubble's distance (340 miles). The transition must signal we're comparing these two very different positions.

steps.title: Match the comparison signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast/comparison. The blank needs a transition signaling that Hubble's distance is being set against JWST's for comparison.
  • Choice A — "Next,": signals the next step in a sequence, but Hubble's position isn't a step that follows from JWST's — they're simultaneous facts being contrasted. ✗
  • Choice B — "Moreover,": signals additional information in the same direction, but Hubble's short distance doesn't reinforce JWST's great distance — it contrasts it. ✗
  • Choice C — "In comparison,": signals that one thing is being measured against another, so fits the contrast between the two telescopes' distances. ✓
  • Choice D — "As a result,": signals a consequence, but Hubble's distance isn't caused by JWST's distance. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
